title: Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy
content:
{{Top|rwm|new|italics=1}} {{Otheruses|Jedi Knight|more=Jedi Academy}} {{VideoGame |image=[[File:Jedi_Academy_PC.png]] |title=''Star Wars: Jedi Knight:
Jedi Academy'' |developer=[[Raven Software]] |publisher=[[LucasArts]] |release date=*[[September 16]], [[2003]] ([[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]]){{WebCite|url=https://www.ign.com/articles/2003/09/16/star-wars-jedi-knight-jedi-academy-review|text=Star Wars Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy Review|author=Butts, Steve|date=2003-09-16|work=[[Wikipedia:IGN|''IGN'']]|archivedate=20200401045956}} *[[November 18]], 2003 ([[Wikipedia:Xbox|Xbox]]) *[[March 26]], 2020 ([[Wikipedia:Nintendo Switch|Nintendo Switch]], [[Wikipedia:PlayStation 4|PlayStation 4]]) |closed= |upc= |engine=[[Wikipedia:id Tech 3|Quake III Arena engine]] |genre=First-person shooter |modes=*Single player *Multiplayer |ratings=*[[Wikipedia:Entertainment Software Rating Board|ESRB]]: Teen (T) *[[Wikipedia:Unterhaltungssoftware Selbstkontrolle|USK]]: 16 |platforms=*[[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]] *[[Wikipedia:MacOS|Mac]] *[[Wikipedia:Xbox|Xbox]] *[[Wikipedia:Nintendo Switch|Nintendo Switch]] *[[Wikipedia:PlayStation 4|PlayStation 4]] |series=''[[Dark Forces/Jedi Knight]]'' |basegame= |expansions= |director= |producer= |designer= |programmer= |artist= |writer= |composer= |timeline=[[14 ABY/Legends|14 ABY]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' |prev=''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast]]'' |next= }} '''''Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy''''' is a ''[[Star Wars]]'' video game played in [[Wikipedia:first-person|first person]] and third person, and is part of the ''Jedi Knight'' series, although it is the first one in which [[Kyle Katarn]] is a non-playable character. Released [[September 16]], [[2003]] ([[Wikipedia:Personal computer|PC]]) and [[November 18]], 2003 ([[Wikipedia:Xbox|Xbox]]), it was developed by [[Raven Software]] and published, distributed and marketed by [[LucasArts]] in [[Wikipedia:North America|North America]] and by [[Wikipedia:Activision|Activision]] in the rest of the world. On [[September 4]], [[2019]], it was announced that ''Jedi Academy'' would be remastered, and it was released for [[Wikipedia:Nintendo Switch|Nintendo Switch]]{{WebCite|url=https://nintendohill.com/2020/03/star-wars-jedi-knight-jedi-academy-for-switch-might-launch-around-march-26-2020|text=Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy For Switch Might Launch Around March 26, 2020|archiveurl=https://archive.ph/3dwaA}} and [[Wikipedia:PlayStation 4|PlayStation 4]] on [[March 26]], [[2020]]. It replaced the cancelled sequel ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight III: Brink of Darkness]]''. ==Opening crawl== {{OpeningCrawl |name=JEDI KNIGHT:
JEDI ACADEMY |text=Ten years after the Battle of Endor, the IMPERIAL REMNANT continues to be driven back by the NEW REPUBLIC, forcing the Imperials to adopt increasingly desperate tactics in the ongoing struggle. Meanwhile, Jedi Master LUKE SKYWALKER moves to restore the JEDI to their formal place as peacekeepers of the galaxy at his JEDI ACADEMY on Yavin 4. JADEN KORR, a promising student from Coruscant, is en route to the Academy after achieving the impossible: creating a lightsaber without any formal Jedi training…}} ==Plot summary== [[Jaden Korr]] and other students were traveling to [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] to begin their training at the [[Jedi Praxeum|Jedi Academy]]. As they were approaching their destination, their [[Yavin Runner II|shuttle]] was damaged and had to make a crash-landing. Korr and [[Rosh Penin]] were separated from the group and had to navigate their way through the jungle. Along the way, they fought some [[Howler (Yavin 4)|howlers]] and encountered the [[Disciples of Ragnos]], a [[Sith cult/Legends|Sith cult]]. Korr also saw the cultists using a [[Scepter of Ragnos|scepter]] to drain [[The Force/Legends|Force]] [[Force energy|energy]] from the [[Great Temple/Legends|Massassi temple]]. When the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] were searching the jungle for the missing students, the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Alora (Dark Jedi)|Alora]] broke into [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]'s personal quarters and stole a record of places where Skywalker had felt a strong connection to the Force. She gave it to her master, who was later revealed to be [[Tavion Axmis|Tavion]]. At the Jedi Academy, Skywalker assigned Jedi Master [[Kyle Katarn]] to teach Korr and Penin. After completing his basic training, Korr, as a [[Jedi Initiate/Legends|Jedi Initiate]], went on missions to [[Mission to Mos Eisley (Disciples of Ragnos crisis)|Mos Eisley]], [[Mission to Tatooine (Disciples of Ragnos crisis)|Tatooine]], [[Mission to Bakura (Disciples of Ragnos crisis)|Bakura]], [[Mission to Blenjeel|Blenjeel]], and [[Mission to Corellia (Disciples of Ragnos crisis)|Corellia]] to investigate cult activities. During this time, he learned that the Sith cult was working closely with the [[Imperial Remnant]]. After the five missions, Skywalker sent Korr to [[Mission to Hoth (Jaden Korr)|Hoth]] to investigate the [[Lanteel Glacier/Legends|site]] where he had a vision of [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]. Although Korr did not sense any Force energy at the location, he discovered that the Imperial Remnant was also on Hoth. While exploring the ruins of [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]], Korr encountered Alora and dueled her before she escaped. Around the same time, Penin went missing during his [[Mission to Byss (Rosh Penin)|mission to Byss]]. Korr was promoted to [[Padawan/Legends|Apprentice]] for his achievements on Hoth, and he progressed to the next stage of training. He completed another five missions to [[Mission to Nar Kreeta|Nar Kreeta]], [[Mission to Zonju V|Zonju V]], [[Mission to Kril'Dor|Kril'Dor]], [[Second mission to Coruscant (Disciples of Ragnos crisis)|Coruscant]], and [[Mission to Dosuun|Dosuun]]. In the meantime, Skywalker had discovered that the cultists had been draining Force energy from the locations mentioned in his record. He sent all available Jedi to investigate those places and directed them to travel in pairs—as a precaution after Penin's disappearance. Korr and Katarn were sent to check out [[Bast Castle|Darth Vader's castle]] on [[Mission to Vjun (Disciples of Ragnos crisis)|Vjun]]. While exploring the castle, they destroyed a power generator to cut a path to the upper levels. However, the ensuing explosion separated Korr from Katarn. Korr moved on alone into the [[Meditation chamber/Legends|meditation chamber]], where he encountered the [[Kothos twins]] and Penin, who had turned to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]]. He killed the Kothos twins and dueled Penin until Katarn caught up and stopped them. At the same moment, Tavion showed up and used [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]] to temporarily incapacitate Katarn and Korr, revealing herself to be Penin's new master and the leader of the cult. To break the stalemate, she used the Force to pull [[Jaden Korr's first lightsaber|Korr's lightsaber]] away from him before destroying it with the scepter and collapsing the ceiling. Tavion and Penin then fled. Katarn used the Force to hold up the rubble while Korr cut a hole in the ground with [[Kyle Katarn's third lightsaber|Katarn's lightsaber]], and the two of them jumped through the hole safely to the level below. Upon returning to the Jedi Academy, Katarn and Korr reported their findings to Skywalker. For displaying courage and skill in defeating the Dark Jedi, Korr was promoted to [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] and he constructed a [[Jaden Korr's second lightsaber|new lightsaber]] to replace the one he lost. Katarn sensed Korr's anger at Penin's betrayal and warned him that his anger could lead him to the dark side. While Skywalker and Katarn tried to find out what Tavion intended to do with the scepter, Korr completed another five missions to [[Mission to Chandrila|Chandrila]], [[Mission to Taanab|Tanaab]], [[Mission to Yalara|Yalara]], [[Mission to Byss (Kyle Katarn)|Byss]], and [[Mission to Ord Mantell (Disciples of Ragnos crisis)|Ord Mantell]]. After Korr finished the fifth mission, Skywalker gathered all the Jedi and told them that he suspected that the cultists wanted to use the Force energy stored in the scepter to resurrect [[Marka Ragnos]], an ancient [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]]. As the Jedi prepared to go to [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] to stop the cult, Katarn told Korr that he had received a distress call from Penin, who was being held captive in an Imperial facility on [[Mission to Taspir III|Taspir III]]. When Korr raised concerns that Penin could still be with the dark side and was trying to lure them into a trap, Katarn told Korr that he would not abandon Penin even if it were a trap. Korr then reluctantly followed Katarn to Taspir III, where they split up at the facility to find Penin. Korr was the first to find Penin, who apologized to him and implored him to bring him back to the Jedi Academy. However, Korr became furious because he thought that Penin had lured him into a trap, so he ignited his lightsaber and pointed it at Penin. While Alora tried to instigate Korr to kill Penin and join the dark side, Katarn used the Force to [[Telepathy/Legends|communicate telepathically]] with Korr and persuade him to spare Penin. Korr realized his mistake and let go of his anger; he kept his lightsaber and tried to leave with Penin. Alora attacked them and cut off Penin's arm. Korr then dueled Alora and eventually slew her. Katarn showed up and told Korr to leave for Korriban first; he would catch up later after bringing Penin to seek medical attention. Korr landed on Korriban and joined the other Jedi in fighting the cultists while navigating his way through the Sith catacombs. He entered the [[Tomb of Marka Ragnos]] and saw Tavion using the scepter to transfer Force energy into the tomb. After Korr defeated Tavion in a lightsaber duel, Ragnos' spirit rose from the tomb and possessed Tavion. The possessed Tavion unsheathed a [[Sith sword]] hidden in the scepter and used it to fight Korr. Korr eventually defeated the possessed Tavion and destroyed the scepter with his lightsaber. Ragnos' spirit left Tavion's body and swore vengeance before returning to his tomb, leaving only a dead Tavion behind. After exiting the tomb, Jaden sealed the entrance and met Skywalker and Katarn on the way out, and they returned to the Jedi Academy together. In the meantime, New Republic [[MC80 Liberty Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|Star Cruisers]] attacked and destroyed the Imperial [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] providing support to the cultists, ending the [[Battle of Korriban (Disciples of Ragnos crisis)|Battle of Korriban]]. Korr was honored as a valuable member of the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and continued to go on missions to maintain peace in the galaxy. On the other hand, a redeemed Penin was accepted back into the Jedi Academy and got a [[Cybernetics/Legends|prosthetic arm]]. ===Non-canon dark-side ending=== Korr can turn to the dark side by killing Penin in the Imperial facility on Taspir III. After igniting his lightsaber through Penin's body, Korr cast him aside and declared that he wanted the scepter and its power for himself. He then dueled Alora and killed her before leaving for Korriban. Katarn showed up too late and reassured a dying Penin that he was a Jedi; Penin died in peace. Katarn then rushed to Korriban to find Korr and stop him. On Korriban, Korr attacked and killed everyone who stood in his way. He entered Ragnos' tomb and killed Tavion in a lightsaber duel. Just as he was about to take the scepter, Katarn showed up and urged him to resist the lure of the dark side and end the conflict. Korr refused and engaged his former master in a lightsaber duel. Toward the end of the duel, Korr used the Force to grab the scepter and [[Force blast|blast Force energy]] at Katarn, knocking him out. He then used the scepter to make a pile of rocks fall on Katarn, and blasted a hole in the wall to make his way out of the tomb. A while later, Skywalker came in and saved Katarn, who told him that Korr had killed Penin and turned to the dark side. Skywalker said he could still sense good in Korr; Katarn left to find Korr in an attempt to turn him back. Meanwhile, Korr boarded the Imperial Star Destroyer, killed its commander, and seized command of the ship. ==Gameplay== ===Overview=== The game is powered by the [[Wikipedia:Quake III Arena|''Quake III Arena'']] graphics engine, with modifications to give a third-person view and new lightsaber effects. Players now have the ability to construct their own lightsaber by choosing the hilt style and color. After completing certain objectives, new saber styles become available including the ability to wield [[Jar'Kai/Legends|dual lightsabers]] as used by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] during the closing fight in [[Star Wars: Episode II Attack of the Clones|''Star Wars'': Episode II ''Attack of the Clones'']] (but first seen in ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II]]'' by the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Boc Aseca]]), or the double-bladed lightsaber made famous by [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] in [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']] and first used by [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]] in [[Star Wars: Tales of the Jedi (comic series)|''Tales of the Jedi'']]. ===Cheats=== Using a cheat code, players can have level 4 Mind trick (normally the highest level is 3). Using this, players can take direct control of an NPC. Other NPCs ignore the captured NPC; however, Jaden is susceptible to attack and players cannot fight back until the player regains control of Jaden by waiting until the Mind trick time limit expires, killing the NPC (this can be done by either throwing them off a cliff or shooting at Jaden if the player has saber defend on 3), or by pressing the Mind trick command key again which immediately cancels the level 4 Mind trick. Also, when using mind trick, the screen looks the same as when controlling a droid in ''Jedi Outcast''. Controlling droids is also possible, as well as various NPCs, using a cheat code. Specifically, after enabling the cheats (by typing 'helpusobi 1' in the console), merely spawn an NPC with a name. (Example: "npc spawn stormtrooper bob") This will spawn a stormtrooper NPC named bob. After this, you will be able to control the NPC as long as it has a name, with the Control cheat code. This also works with droid NPCs. (Example: "control bob") ==Continuity== The main character of the game, Jaden Korr, was named [[Atton Rand]] in early drafts. The name was later used in ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]''. One of Atton's in-game [[Easter egg (virtual)|Easter egg]] lines is a joke about him being added to the game last minute, and how he was supposed to have been in "a spin-off to ''Jedi Knight''." ===Aurebesh transliteration=== [[File:Many_ways_exist_to_lose_your_money.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Many ways exist to lose your money here...many...]] *The title screen of ''Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy'' shows [[Aurebesh/Legends|Aurebesh]] characters in the background which transliterates to: {{Quote|Star Wars Jedi Knight Jedi Academy Developed By Raven Software Published By Lucasarts Entertainment Company Features Create Your Own Jedi Tiered Level System New MP Game Siege New Sabers New Force Powers Arioch Terrain System New Enemies And more Coming Summer 2003 And yes this really does say something if you translate it.|''Jedi Academy'' title screen|''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy]]''}} *In the mission to [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], when you release the ''[[Raven's Claw]]'' and the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']] from the beams, [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] shows up from the exit to tell you to follow. The door reads ''SALIDA'' (Spanish for "exit") in reversed Aurebesh. *Outside [[Lannik Racto]]'s office, the inscription on the gate reads ''OFFICE''. The small yellow brown pillar reads just ''Pillar''. *Numerous objects bear labels that translate to literal descriptions of the object. On [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]], for example, large crates are labeled ''HOTH CRATE'' and chairs in the [[Echo Base/Legends|Echo Base]] command center are labeled ''REBEL CHAIR''. *During the [[Second mission to Coruscant (Disciples of Ragnos crisis)|Second Mission to Coruscant]], a number of billboards flank the path taken by Jaden Korr. One of them reads, "Many ways exist to lose your money here...many..." *The prisoners wear clothes with text saying "CREW." *In the main menu, things such as Load or Options are also written in aurebesh if highlighted with mouse. ===Other changes from ''Jedi Outcast'' to ''Jedi Academy''=== *The player's character, Jaden, is customizable, and players have the option of being male or female of different species including a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] female, [[Kel Dor/Legends|Kel Dor]] male, [[Zabrak/Legends|Zabrak]] female or [[Rodian/Legends|Rodian]] male (as well as [[Human/Legends|Human]] of either gender). You can also choose from several lightsaber hilts and blade and clothes colors (Skin Color For the Twi'lek). *Halfway through the game, players can choose to wield one lightsaber, two lightsabers, or a [[Double-bladed lightsaber/Legends|double-bladed lightsaber]]. The latter two can both be wielded as single blades; the player can select to ignite only one of the two blades interchanging between one and two whenever they wish. *There is a mission selection screen with five missions on it. You can decide which one you want to do. After you have completed four it asks you if you want to complete the fifth or skip it. Then there is another story mission, and then five more missions. *New moves including katas, wall gripping, and acrobatics, which add a new level to combat, instead of the standard slashing and cleaving. Depending on your type of lightsaber(s), you can have additional defensive and offensive moves. *Two new weapons: [[DL-44 heavy blaster pistol/Legends|DL-44 heavy blaster pistol]] and [[Stouker concussion rifle]]. *New gametype: ''Powerduel''—Players join as an individual or as a team of two. The individual has more health and Force power in exchange for being at a disadvantage in numbers. *Siege Mode: Players work to complete goals and stop the other team from completing their goals. *Enhanced rendering of the Quake 3 Engine. *Force powers are now selectable, and you can't learn all of them. Instead, you gain your universal Force powers automatically, but with each mission you choose to add a point to either a light- or dark-side-themed Force Power. New powers include [[Tutaminis|Force absorb]] and [[Force barrier/Legends|Force protection]], [[Force drain/Legends|Drain Life]], [[Force Enrage|Force rage]], and [[Force sense/Legends|Force sense]]. All of the classic powers ([[Telekinesis/Legends|Force Push]], Force Pull, [[Force jump/Legends|Force Jump]], [[Force speed/Legends|Force speed]], [[Mind trick/Legends|Mind trick]], [[Telekinesis/Legends|Force Choke]], [[Force lightning/Legends|Force lightning]]), are still selectable. *Force Sense can now be used in story mode, and is not just a multiplayer exclusive. It is even needed to solve puzzles. ==Legacy== Following [[The Walt Disney Company]]'s decision to close LucasArts on [[April 3]], 2013, the developers at Raven Software released the source code for the game on SourceForge under GNU GPLv2 licensing. However, the source code for both games disappeared from SourceForge without explanation. SourceForge later explained that Raven had requested the removal of the code, which may have been due to the presence of proprietary code that was not intended to be made public.{{WebCite|url=https://www.kotaku.com.au/2013/04/just-like-that-jedi-knight-code-has-been-removed-from-sourceforge|text=Just Like That, Jedi Knight Code Has Been Removed From SourceForge|author=Booker, Logan|date=2013-04-13|work=[[Wikipedia:Kotaku|Kotaku]]|archivedate=20201020025223}} Later, based on the source code released by Raven, a group of coders by the name of JACoders created OpenJK, an effort to improve the game engine, without any modification to gameplay, for both ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Knight II: Jedi Outcast]]'' (single player) and ''Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Jedi Academy'' (single and multiplayer), maintaining full backwards compatibility with the existing games.{{WebCite|url=https://github.com/JACoders/OpenJK|text=JACoders / OpenJK|work=[[Wikipedia:GitHub|GitHub]]|archivedate=20210225011818}} ==Credits== {{IncompleteCredits}} {{Credits |cast= *'''[[Philip Tanzini]]''' as '''[[Jaden Korr]]''' (Male) *'''[[Jennifer
